target.title: fix(reply): clarify provider internal error copy
target.body:
Supersedes #93597 

## Summary
- Classify provider internal errors that occur before any reply as provider-internal failures.
- Surface a specific user-facing message that recommends retrying or switching models.
- Avoid the generic `/new` session-reset guidance for provider-side temporary errors.

## Problem
A real Telegram turn failed before reply when the selected provider returned a generic upstream `server_error`:

```text
[openai-transport] [responses] error provider=fyapis api=openai-responses model=gpt-5.5 code=server_error type=server_error message=An error occurred while processing your request...
[model-fallback/decision] decision=candidate_failed requested=fyapis/gpt-5.5 candidate=fyapis/gpt-5.5 reason=timeout next=none
[telegram] outbound message: ⚠️ Something went wrong while processing your request. Please try again, or use /new to start a fresh session.
```

The session state was not corrupt; `/new` was misleading. The actual recovery is to retry later or switch to another model/provider when no runtime fallback candidate is configured.

This is distinct from #45050. That issue covered raw provider payloads leaking to channel users; current main already sanitizes the raw payload. The remaining problem is that the sanitized internal-error copy falls through to the generic external-run failure text.

## Fix
`classifyProviderRequestError()` now maps these provider internal-error shapes to a dedicated user-facing message:

```text
⚠️ The model provider returned a temporary internal error before replying. Try again in a moment, or switch to another model if it keeps happening.
```

The classifier covers:

- `The AI service returned an internal error...`
- `provider returned an internal error...`
- generic provider runtime messages that also include `server_error` or `internal error`

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/provider-request-error-classifier.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts --testNamePattern 'provider internal errors|generic server_error|HTTP 429 failures before reply'`
  - `4 passed`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/provider-request-error-classifier.ts src/auto-reply/reply/provider-request-error-classifier.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`
  - passed
- `git diff --check`
  - passed

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** A provider-side temporary internal error before any assistant reply should not be reported as a session-reset problem. The visible reply should tell the user to retry later or switch models instead of suggesting `/new`.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Linux, using the same provider-internal sanitized error text observed in the Telegram incident.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
`pnpm exec tsx --eval "import { classifyProviderRequestError } from './src/auto-reply/reply/provider-request-error-classifier.ts'; const result = classifyProviderRequestError(new Error('The AI service returned an internal error. Please try again in a moment.')); console.log(JSON.stringify(result, null, 2)); console.log('containsNewHint=' + String(result?.userMessage.includes('/new')));"`

**Evidence after fix:**
```text
{
  "code": "provider_internal_error",
  "userMessage": "⚠️ The model provider returned a temporary internal error before replying. Try again in a moment, or switch to another model if it keeps happening.",
  "technicalMessage": "The AI service returned an internal error. Please try again in a moment."
}
containsNewHint=false
```

**Observed result after fix:** The same sanitized provider internal-error text now maps to the provider-internal copy and does not include `/new` guidance.

**What was not tested:** I did not force another live provider `server_error` from the production Telegram channel. The after-fix proof uses the exact sanitized error string captured from the live incident, without sending another external model request.

## Risk
Low. The change only affects pre-reply provider request failures that already reached the provider-error classifier. Existing conversation-state and HTTP 429 handling remain unchanged.

### Verification refresh — 2026-06-19

Refreshed the PR branch onto current `origin/main` after the ClawSweeper review noted the unrelated `check-test-types` failure. New head: `8265fc71f30a556a2f17ca1401b1c8b8a6f3198d`.

Local verification after the rebase:

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/provider-request-error-classifier.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`
  - `provider-request-error-classifier.test.ts`: 12 passed
  - `agent-runner-execution.test.ts`: 205 passed
- `pnpm check:test-types`

Observed result after fix: the provider internal-error classifier tests and agent-runner external reply regression remain green after rebasing, and the local test typecheck that previously blocked the PR now passes on the refreshed branch.

GitHub Actions note: the refreshed fork PR run currently needs maintainer workflow approval before the required upstream CI lanes can execute.

target.title: Strip apiKey fields from generated models.json
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: generated `models.json` could persist `apiKey` fields from provider config rows.
- Solution: strip `apiKey` recursively from the final `models.json` write payload.
- What changed: provider/model metadata still writes, but provider-level and nested `apiKey` fields are omitted; tests now assert this contract.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): runtime auth resolution and provider discovery credential lookup remain outside the generated file.

## Motivation

- Prevent model config auth material from being persisted in a file that agent/runtime readers may inspect.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #11829
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: generated `models.json` no longer includes provider-level or nested `apiKey` fields that could be exposed to agent/runtime readers.
- Real environment tested: local Windows checkout using Node v24.14.0 from the Codex bundled runtime because the system Node v22.15.0 is below the repo requirement.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the real `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` path against a temporary OpenClaw agent dir with a configured provider containing fake provider-level and model-level `apiKey` values, then read the generated `models.json`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied live terminal output:

```text
models_path=C:\Users\CAMILO\Documents\Codex\2026-05-20\you-are-my-autonomous-public-work\openclaw\.artifacts\proof-agent\models.json
provider_present=true
provider_apiKey_present=false
model_apiKey_present=false
secret_string_present=false
model_secret_string_present=false
model_id=proof-model
```

- Observed result after fix: provider rows, base URLs, headers, and model metadata still write normally, while `apiKey` is omitted from `models.json`.
- What was not tested: full repository test suite and live end-to-end agent prompt inspection.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): not captured for this generated-file security hardening path; after-fix copied live output above shows the generated file no longer contains provider or model apiKey fields.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `planOpenClawModelsJsonWithDeps` serialized the fully merged provider config directly, including auth fields that were needed for discovery/runtime setup but not safe to persist in `models.json`.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests asserted auth markers were persisted instead of asserting generated-file redaction.
- Contributing context (if known): the existing pipeline already normalized several secret references, but final write serialization had no dedicated `apiKey` removal step.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts`, `src/agents/models-config.runtime-source-snapshot.test.ts`, `src/agents/models-config.skips-writing-models-json-no-env-token.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: generated `models.json` keeps provider/model metadata but omits provider-level and nested `apiKey` fields.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it exercises the write-plan output directly and existing generated-file cases that previously expected auth markers.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Generated `models.json` no longer contains `apiKey` properties.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? Yes, `apiKey` fields are stripped from generated `models.json`.
- New/changed network calls? No

## Verification

- `C:\Users\CAMILO\.cache\codex-runtimes\codex-primary-runtime\dependencies\node\bin\node.exe scripts\run-vitest.mjs src/agents/models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts src/agents/models-config.runtime-source-snapshot.test.ts src/agents/models-config.skips-writing-models-json-no-env-token.test.ts --run`

target.title: fix(feishu): use middle-dot separator in card note footer
target.body:
Feishu's card markdown renderer parses 'Agent: name' as definition-list syntax and hoists the agent name to the top of the rendered message. Switch the key/value separator from ': ' to ' · ' so the footer stays in the footer.

target.title: fix(ui): improve Arabic Control UI translations
target.body:
## Summary
- Improve Arabic Control UI translations for skills, browser/tool profile labels, chat controls, and cron empty states.
- Align the Arabic glossary with the locale/TM decision for `Skills -> المهارات` so future i18n syncs preserve the term.
- Keep the Arabic translation memory file in sync with the locale bundle.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Arabic Control UI strings now render in Arabic for the updated skills, chat, and Cron surfaces instead of showing English fallbacks or inconsistent terminology.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 11, local OpenClaw gateway on `127.0.0.1:18789`, Control UI dev server from this PR branch with Vite at `http://127.0.0.1:5174/`, opened in Microsoft Edge through Playwright.
- **Exact steps or command run after the patch:** Started the UI with `pnpm --dir ui exec vite --host 127.0.0.1 --port 5174`, opened the page in a real browser, set `openclaw.i18n.locale` to `ar`, authenticated with the local gateway password, then navigated through chat, Skills, and Cron.
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied live output from the real browser page after reload and navigation:

```text
[chat]
الدردشة
الدردشة
الدردشة
المهارات
إرفاق ملف
بدء المحادثة الصوتية
إرسال

[skills]
›
›
المهارات
المهارات
المهارات
المهارات ومفاتيح API.
تحديث
All58
Ready13
Needs Setup45

[cron]
مهام Cron
مهام Cron
مهام Cron
الإيقاظات والتشغيلات المتكررة.
لا توجد مهام مجدولة بعد.
أنشئ مهمة من مطالبة بلغة بسيطة؛ يمكن تأجيل الحقول المتقدمة.
لا توجد تشغيلات مطابقة.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The live Control UI rendered readable Arabic for the changed surfaces, including `المهارات`, `المهارات ومفاتيح API.`, `إرسال`, `إرفاق ملف`, `لا توجد مهام مجدولة بعد.`, and `أنشئ مهمة من مطالبة بلغة بسيطة`.
- **What was not tested:** None

## Validation

Local note: `pnpm test:ui` did not fully complete in this WSL environment because Playwright browser tests need the missing system library `libnspr4.so`; the targeted i18n test passed with the repository Vitest config. A Windows browser proof was run separately above.

target.title: fix(cli): harden config and command validation
target.body:
## Summary
- harden operator-facing CLI validation for configure sections, channel log filters/line limits, agent timeouts, session active filters, and OpenRouter scan counts
- improve setup/config diagnostics for invalid non-interactive setup, malformed include JSON, and root-level env substitution failures
- add regression coverage for the tightened parser/error paths

## Links
Closes: none.
Supersedes: none found.
Related:
- https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/16351
- https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/74575
- https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/50274

## Verification
- Testbox `tbx_01krwcxengn7r9cnadeq0fh4vs`, head `9e34dce4bee53b1970486bd8c8460150212cb8ff`
- `pnpm test:serial src/commands/configure.commands.test.ts src/config/includes.test.ts src/commands/onboard-non-interactive.invalid-config.test.ts src/config/env-substitution.test.ts src/commands/channels.logs.test.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts src/commands/sessions.test.ts src/commands/models/scan.test.ts` passed: 8 files, 124 tests
- `pnpm check:changed` passed: lanes `core, coreTests, docs`, changed count 17
- Autoreview passed after follow-up fix: `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --reviewer codex --fallback-reviewer none --output /tmp/openclaw-config-command-autoreview-after-fix.txt`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: invalid operator CLI/config inputs now fail explicitly instead of silently falling back or partially parsing.
Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox for `openclaw/openclaw` branch `impact/config-command-operator-flow`, head `9e34dce4bee53b1970486bd8c8460150212cb8ff`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: targeted command/config Vitest files followed by `pnpm check:changed` in Testbox.
Evidence after fix: targeted tests passed with 124 assertions and `check:changed` completed with zero errors.
Observed result after fix: malformed values such as unknown channel log filters, fractional/blank numeric options, and malformed active filters now hit explicit errors before doing the operator action.
What was not tested: live provider calls, live gateway/channel traffic, and package artifact install paths.

## Risk
- stricter CLI validation can break scripts that accidentally relied on malformed values being truncated or ignored
- no intended public config shape changes, auth boundary changes, env precedence changes, or migration behavior changes

commands.author: wangwllu

target.title: fix(announce-delivery): backfill effectiveDirectOrigin.to from requester session entry
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a silent media-delivery drop when `image_generate` (or any
generated-media task) is invoked off the direct-reply path — heartbeat, cron,
subagent spawn, etc. — where `agentTo` is undefined.

In that case the persisted `requesterOrigin` carries `channel` and
`accountId` but no `to`, and every downstream `Boolean(channel && to)`
deliverability gate short-circuits. The artifact is generated and written to
disk, but never delivered; `task_runs.status` is then marked failed with
`completion delivery failed after successful generation`. User sees nothing.

The fix is a one-merge change in
`src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts`: move the already-loaded
`requesterEntry` slightly earlier, derive a delivery-context fallback from it
via the existing `deliveryContextFromSession`, and merge that into
`completionExternalFallbackOrigin` *before* `effectiveDirectOrigin` is
computed.

The in-source comment at that exact site (previously at lines 1202–1205)
already named this gap; the patch makes the comment accurate by acting on it.

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#86034 (Hypothesis A only — origin missing `to`).

## What the patch does

- `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts` — Move the `loadRequesterSessionEntry` call up; compute `requesterSessionDeliveryFallback = deliveryContextFromSession(requesterEntry)`; merge it into the existing fallback chain.
- `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` — New regression: completion origin missing `to`, requester session entry has `lastTo` → `effectiveDirectOrigin.to` is populated and external delivery is selected (`deliver: true`).

Total non-test diff: ~14 lines.

## Risk / blast radius

Low. The merge happens inside an `expectsCompletionMessage` branch that
already documents this exact intent. `mergeDeliveryContext` refuses to cross
channels, so we cannot synthesize a delivery target that wasn't already in the
requester session entry. Happy-path tests
(`subagent-registry-lifecycle`, `image-generate-tool`,
`image-generation-task-status`) confirm no regression when the origin already
carries `to`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: When `image_generate` is invoked off the direct-reply path (heartbeat / cron / subagent spawn) on a Telegram surface, the generated artifact lands on disk but is never delivered to the chat; the user sees nothing while `task_runs.status` is marked failed with `completion delivery failed after successful generation`. After this patch, on the same Telegram path, the generated image is delivered to the chat.

Real environment tested: Live Telegram Desktop end-to-end via `openclaw-mantis` workflow, executed against a real OpenClaw setup driving a real Telegram client. Two runs were captured side-by-side:
- Baseline: `main` at `6d5061c2…` (pre-patch tip), real Telegram Desktop transport.
- Candidate: this PR head at `974b07b0…` (the original PR head Mantis observed before today's force-push rebase to `f6a45b1f`; the patch hunk is identical, so the observed transport-level behavior carries over). Real Telegram Desktop transport.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
# Live Telegram Desktop end-to-end proof was driven by openclaw-mantis.
# The Mantis workflow run that captured this PR's real-behavior evidence:
gh run view 26902454818 --repo openclaw/openclaw
gh api repos/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26902454818/artifacts

# Local supplemental regression suite on the patched head (f6a45b1f):
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts \
  src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts \
  src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts \
  src/agents/image-generation-task-status.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:
- Mantis live Telegram Desktop run: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26902454818
- Mantis artifacts (recordings / screenshots / MP4s side-by-side): https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26902454818/artifacts/7393384873
- Mantis comment with embedded GIFs / screenshots / MP4s is in this PR thread.

```text
Mantis run 26902454818 verdicts (live Telegram Desktop transport):
- Scenario:  telegram-desktop-proof
  Baseline (main 6d5061c2…):       pass — expected baseline visual proof captured
  Candidate (PR head 974b07b0…):   pass — expected candidate visual proof captured
  Overall:                         true
```

Observed result after fix: On the previously broken off-direct-reply path the Mantis-driven Telegram Desktop client visibly received the generated image. Local supplemental regression: `subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` (with the new regression case), `media-generate-background-shared.test.ts`, `subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts`, `image-generate-tool.test.ts`, `image-generation-task-status.test.ts` — all 5 files, 194 tests, green on the patched head. The new regression case in `subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` fails without the patch and passes with it; it asserts that when the completion origin is missing `to` and `requesterEntry.lastTo` is populated, `effectiveDirectOrigin.to` is backfilled and `deliver: true` is selected — exactly the gate that was short-circuiting in the reported bug.

What was not tested: Crabbox / Testbox suites and `tsgo` typecheck were skipped per repo guidance for this checkout. No live Telegram Bot API account other than the Mantis-driven Telegram Desktop scenario was exercised. Hypothesis B (the `wakeTaskCompletion` retry asymmetry described under Follow-up) is intentionally out of scope for this PR.

## Follow-up (Hypothesis B)

Hypothesis B from the review — `wakeTaskCompletion → false` does not retry
`tryDeliverMediaGenerationDirect` (only the `throw` branch does), and the
in-wake fallback gates on `MEDIA_DIRECT_FALLBACK_DELIVERY_REASONS` which
excludes `requester_abandoned` and the `deliver=false` reason from Hypothesis
A — is a real but separable robustness gap. It crosses the larger
`MEDIA_DIRECT_FALLBACK_DELIVERY_REASONS` contract surface and warrants a
"delivery contract decision" maintainer review on its own. See
`src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.ts:425-468` for the
asymmetric branch.

Fixing A alone unblocks the reported Telegram repro: once `origin.to` is
populated, the in-wake direct fallback (which also checks `origin.to`)
likewise becomes viable.

target.title: #94655: doctor: cron store issues finding persists after --fix and references migrated jobs.json
target.body:
## Summary

`openclaw doctor` shows "Cron store issues" when cron jobs have unresolved shell/process tool prompts that require manual command conversion. The finding includes "Repair with `openclaw doctor --fix`" and references `~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json`, but `--fix` is a no-op for these manual-only warnings and the live store is SQLite-backed, not a JSON file.

Two issues fixed:
1. Doctor now separates **auto-repairable** from **manual-only** warnings. Only repairable items prompt `--fix`; manual conversion warnings are shown for information without the misleading repair hint.
2. The store path heading now clarifies the store is SQLite-backed and the path is a logical key, not a live file.

## Changes

- **`src/commands/doctor/cron/index.ts`**: Add `hasRepairableItems` check to conditionally show "Repair with doctor --fix" only when `--fix` can actually do something. Update store path heading to clarify SQLite-backed storage.
- **`src/commands/doctor/cron/index.test.ts`**: Add regression test verifying manual-only warnings are shown without `--fix` prompt.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:**
Separate manual-only conversion warnings from `--fix` repair hint in doctor cron store issues display. Update store path heading to clarify SQLite-backed logical key.

**Real environment tested:**
- OS: Linux 4.19.112 (x86_64)
- Runtime: Node v24.13.1, pnpm 10.10.0
- Setup: OpenClaw local dev worktree, real cron doctor code from openclaw repo at 65e77b82f5

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:**
1. Create a test cron job with an `agentTurn` payload containing a shell/process-like message that cannot be auto-converted (e.g. "run a python script to process the daily report")
2. Run `maybeRepairLegacyCronStore` with the test job in the store
3. Before fix: doctor shows "Repair with `openclaw doctor --fix`" and prompts for repair, but `--fix` does nothing
4. After fix: doctor shows the manual conversion warning without the `--fix` prompt and does not prompt for repair
5. Run the full test suite: 29 tests pass confirming the new behavior

**Evidence after fix:**
```
$ grep -n "hasRepairableItems\|logical store key\|manual conversion and cannot" src/commands/doctor/cron/index.ts
371:  const hasRepairableItems =
385:  : \`Cron store issues detected (logical store key: \${shortenHomePath(storePath)}, SQLite-backed).\`;
391:    message.push(
395:    \`These warnings require manual conversion and cannot be resolved with \${formatCliCommand("openclaw doctor --fix")}.\`,
```

```
$ pnpm test -- --run src/commands/doctor/cron/index.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  29 passed (29)
   Start at  02:01:53
   Duration  2.96s (transform 1.09s, setup 565ms, import 1.40s, tests 623ms, environment 0ms)
```

Test for the new behavior:
```
✓ shows manual conversion warning without --fix prompt for unresolved shell tool prompt (#94655)
```

**Observed result after fix:**

Before (broken):
```
Cron store issues detected at ~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json.
- 1 job asks an isolated agent for shell/process tools and needs manual command conversion: `Shell-job`
Repair with `openclaw doctor --fix` to normalize the store before the next scheduler run.
```
`--fix` does nothing but is still suggested.

After (fixed):
```
Cron store issues detected (logical store key: ~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json, SQLite-backed).
- 1 job asks an isolated agent for shell/process tools and needs manual command conversion: `Shell-job`
These warnings require manual conversion and cannot be resolved with `openclaw doctor --fix`.
```
No repair prompt; path clarified as SQLite-backed logical key.

**What was not tested:** End-to-end doctor output rendering with actual SQLite store (test uses in-memory store simulation via `writeCurrentCronStore`). No runtime cron behavior, storage, or execution logic is changed.

target.title: fix(cli): sync official plugins during update --all
target.body:
## Summary

- Pass the existing official-plugin sync flag when `openclaw plugins update --all` invokes the npm plugin updater.
- Carry the configured `update.channel` into that bulk official-sync path so beta-channel users stay on the beta release line instead of being silently normalized to stable/latest.
- Keep targeted `openclaw plugins update <id>` behavior unchanged so operator-authored specs are not silently rewritten.
- Add CLI coverage proving `--all` enables `syncOfficialPluginInstalls` with beta channel context while manual plugin updates still leave both values unset.

## Linked context

Closes #94083

Related context: this came from a Windows stable-upgrade case where exact `2026.6.8-beta.1` official plugin install records were reported as up to date after the core moved to `2026.6.8`, while `gateway status` still reported official plugin version drift.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `plugins update --all` could leave a trusted official plugin pinned to an exact beta spec after a stable core upgrade and report it as unchanged instead of converging to the current official catalog.
- Real environment tested: Windows source checkout on this branch, with isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, and npm cache under a temp directory. The temp config seeded one tracked official npm plugin install record: `codex` pinned to exact `@openclaw/codex@2026.6.8-beta.1` with matching beta resolved metadata.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```powershell
node scripts/run-node.mjs plugins update --all --dry-run
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
PROOF_ROOT=C:\Users\ADMINI~1\AppData\Local\Temp\openclaw-proof-plugin-update-all-3cc0f3add9dd4d76a554b2f440f4ed6c
OPENCLAW_HOME=C:\Users\ADMINI~1\AppData\Local\Temp\openclaw-proof-plugin-update-all-3cc0f3add9dd4d76a554b2f440f4ed6c\home
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=C:\Users\ADMINI~1\AppData\Local\Temp\openclaw-proof-plugin-update-all-3cc0f3add9dd4d76a554b2f440f4ed6c\state
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=C:\Users\ADMINI~1\AppData\Local\Temp\openclaw-proof-plugin-update-all-3cc0f3add9dd4d76a554b2f440f4ed6c\openclaw.json5
npm_config_cache=C:\Users\ADMINI~1\AppData\Local\Temp\openclaw-proof-plugin-update-all-3cc0f3add9dd4d76a554b2f440f4ed6c\npm-cache
Would update codex: unknown -> 2026.6.8.
```

- Observed result after fix: The patched real CLI `plugins update --all --dry-run` no longer treats the exact beta official plugin record as simply up to date. It routes the bulk update through official-catalog sync and plans convergence to `2026.6.8`.
- What was not tested: A full packaged beta-to-stable upgrade replay with real npm installs and gateway restart was not run. The proof above uses a real source CLI command against isolated temp OpenClaw state/config. Focused tests now cover both the stable convergence routing and the beta-channel preservation invariant that the updater already supports.

## Tests and validation

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/plugins-cli.update.test.ts --testNamePattern="syncs official catalog specs with beta channel context"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/update.test.ts --testNamePattern="tries npm beta for default npm specs on beta channel and preserves the default selector"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/plugins-cli.update.test.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/cli/plugins-update-command.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.update.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Result: focused CLI beta-channel test passed, focused updater beta-channel test passed, full `plugins-cli.update.test.ts` passed (`28 passed`), oxlint passed, and diff whitespace check passed.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. `plugins update --all` can now move trusted official plugin records from stale exact official specs to the current official catalog target.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No config schema or migration change. Plugin install records may be updated by the existing updater logic when `--all` is used.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Accidentally changing targeted plugin update semantics or dropping beta-channel semantics while enabling bulk official sync.

How is that risk mitigated?

The official-sync flag and `updateChannel` are only set for `params.opts.all`; the manual targeted-update test explicitly asserts both stay unset for `plugins update codex`. The new `update.channel = beta` CLI test proves bulk official sync carries beta context into `updateNpmInstalledPlugins`, and the existing updater beta-channel test proves that context selects the beta npm line.

## Current review state

Updated after ClawSweeper review: the PR now carries configured update-channel context into the `plugins update --all` official-sync path and includes beta-channel regression coverage for the reported blocker.

re-review: mantis reported the native telegram proof could not reliably capture the voice-message typing cue because it was too brief. i changed the voice-only direct-message preflight path to await the telegram `sendchataction(typing)` request before starting audio transcription, so the status request is accepted before stt begins instead of being fire-and-forget.

validation:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.typing.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `git diff --check`
commands.freeform_prompt:
Mantis reported the native Telegram proof could not reliably capture the voice-message typing cue because it was too brief. Validation:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.typing.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `git diff --check`

commands.author: vincentkoc

target.title: fix(skills/1password): stop forcing tmux for desktop app auth (#52540)
target.body:
## Summary

The bundled `1password` skill instructed agents to wrap every `op` invocation in a tmux session. That blanket requirement breaks the **desktop app** integration path: the 1Password CLI desktop-app integration uses a per-user IPC channel (XPC on macOS via the Browser Helper, a Unix domain socket on Linux, a named pipe on Windows) established for the gateway's exec environment, and that channel is not reliably reachable from a freshly-spawned tmux subshell. Agents that follow the skill in desktop-app mode see early steps succeed but later `op` calls fail.

This PR splits the skill guidance by auth mode:

- **Service account** (`OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN` set): run `op` directly, no tmux.
- **Desktop app** (no service account, app integration enabled): run `op` directly so it inherits the host IPC channel.
- **Standalone signin** (CLI-only interactive `op signin --account ...`): keep tmux only here, and fix the example to `eval "$(op signin ...)"`. The previous example printed the eval-style export but never applied it, so subsequent `op whoami`/`op vault list` would fail with no exported `OP_SESSION_*`.

Also corrects the per-platform IPC description in the skill body and `references/get-started.md`: previous text described a single "per-user Unix domain socket" globally, which is misleading on macOS (XPC) and Windows (named pipe).

Rebased onto current `main` (was ~12k commits behind); resolved the `SKILL.md` header conflict and dropped the contributor `CHANGELOG.md` entry per repo policy.

Fixes #52540.

## Real behavior proof

I have a host with paired 1Password **desktop-app** integration, so the desktop-app claim is verified live. All account-identifying data below is redacted.

Behavior addressed: the skill forced `tmux` around every `op` call, which breaks the desktop-app IPC path; the standalone-signin example also failed to `eval` the session export.
Real environment tested: macOS (Darwin 25.5.0), 1Password desktop app with CLI integration enabled, op 2.33.1. The shell was NOT inside tmux ($TMUX unset) and had NO OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN set, exercising the desktop-app path exactly as the rewritten skill describes.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran `op vault list` and `op whoami` directly (no tmux, no service-account token), as the rewritten desktop-app section instructs.
Evidence after fix: redacted terminal output below.

```
$ echo "TMUX=${TMUX:-<unset>}  OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN=${OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN:+set}"
TMUX=<unset>  OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN=<unset>
$ op --version
2.33.1
$ op vault list          # direct exec, no tmux
ID                            NAME
<redacted-id-1>               <redacted-vault-1>
<redacted-id-2>               <redacted-vault-2>
... (8 vaults returned)
$ op whoami              # direct exec, no tmux
URL:        https://<redacted-account>.1password.com/
Email:      <redacted>@<redacted>
User ID:    <redacted-user-id>
```

Observed result after fix: both `op vault list` and `op whoami` succeed via direct exec with no tmux session and no service-account token, confirming the rewritten desktop-app section. Idle-lock nuance: after the desktop app auto-locks, the first `op` call re-triggers system auth; once unlocked, subsequent calls work directly without tmux.
What was not tested: the Linux (Unix-domain-socket) and Windows (named-pipe) desktop-app transports were not exercised on this host; the per-platform mechanism differs but the agent-facing rule (run `op` directly, not via tmux) is identical, and a fresh standalone `op signin` flow was not re-run though the `eval` fix matches documented 1Password behavior.

## Files changed

- `skills/1password/SKILL.md`
- `skills/1password/references/get-started.md`

No `CHANGELOG.md` entry, per repo policy (release-owned). Release-note context: *Skills/1password — stop forcing every `op` call through tmux; detect auth mode (service account / desktop app / standalone signin) and use tmux only for the standalone interactive signin path. Fixes #52540.*

## Risks

Low. Skill-only. Reverts the over-broad tmux requirement on two of three auth-mode paths; the third (standalone signin) keeps tmux and fixes a real `eval` bug. Agents that explicitly want tmux for their own session-recording can still wrap their own commands.

target.title: fix: default cron runMode to "due" instead of "force" (#94270)
target.body:
## Description

When the `runMode` parameter is omitted from a cron "run" action, the default value now respects schedule guards (`"due"`) instead of bypassing them (`"force"`). This prevents unintended execution of scheduled jobs outside their configured time windows. Fixes a P1 security issue where bare cron run bypassed all schedule guards.

## Changes
- **`src/agents/tools/cron-tool.ts`**: 1-line change: default `runMode` fallback from `"force"` to `"due"`
- **`src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts`**: Updated expectations from `mode: "force"` to `mode: "due"`

## Related Issue
Fixes #94270

## AI Assistance
This PR was created with assistance from AI (Claude Code).

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** When callers invoke cron.run without specifying runMode, the default value is now "due" instead of "force". The cron scheduler checks whether the job is due (cron expression matches, job enabled, nextRunAtMs passed) before executing, rather than unconditionally forcing execution.

**Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64 production server, OpenClaw Gateway v1.8.0, Node.js v22.22.0, branch fix/cron-runmode-default-94270.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:**
1. Created a cron job scheduled 2 hours in the future via: `openclaw cron add --name "proof" --at "2h-from-now" --system-event "test" --json`
2. Ran: `openclaw cron run <job-id> --due` (this simulates the fix's default behavior where omitted runMode sends "due")
3. Ran: `openclaw cron run <job-id> --force` (verifies the explicit override escape hatch)

**After-fix evidence:**
```
$ openclaw cron run <job-id> --due
{
  "ok": true,
  "ran": false,
  "reason": "not-due"
}

$ openclaw cron run <job-id> --force
{
  "ok": true,
  "enqueued": true
}
```

**Observed result after fix:**
- Run with --due: Job correctly refused to execute with reason "not-due" because the job was scheduled 2 hours in the future
- Run with --force: Job immediately executed, confirming the explicit force override remains available
- The 1-line change in cron-tool.ts switches the omitted-runMode default from "force" to "due"
- Unit tests: 33/33 pass (2 updated expectations: mode "force" -> mode "due")

**What was not tested:** Full integration test with MCP clients or Gateway-to-Gateway cron forwarding. The Gateway CLI --force flag and Gateway RPC bare cron.run remain unchanged (they still default to force).

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- packed this branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball into a fresh npm project, and verified `npm audit --omit=dev --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`

target.title: fix(barnacle): resync candidate labels on ready_for_review, remove stale labels
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` return value + `removeLabels` call path
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a new coverage gap
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a maintainer-owned PR transitions from draft to `ready_for_review` (or is edited), Barnacle recomputes candidate labels and removes any stale Barnacle-owned candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) that no longer match the PR body/files.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [add missing labels only] -> [stale labels persist]

After:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [recompute labels] -> [add missing + remove stale] -> [labels match body]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No (existing GitHub API calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any (GitHub Actions)
- Runtime/container: Node 24 (GitHub Actions)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: GitHub auto-response workflow

### Steps

1. Open a draft PR with a blank issue template body
2. Barnacle auto-labels it with `triage: blank-template`
3. Edit body to fill in the template (Problem/Why/What)
4. Click "Ready for review"

### Expected

- `triage: blank-template` is removed; correct candidate labels applied

### Actual

- `triage: blank-template` persisted (before fix)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `ready_for_review` with meaningful body removes stale labels; `ready_for_review` recomputes candidate labels; `bad-barnacle` suppresses removal; maintainer-authored PRs not mutated
- Edge cases checked: `bad-barnacle` suppression, maintainer/OWNER/COLLABORATOR skip, mixed candidate label transitions
- What you did **not** verify: Live GitHub workflow run (requires merge to test end-to-end)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Stale label removal could incorrectly strip a label if `classifyPullRequestCandidateLabels` has a bug.
  - Mitigation: Removal only targets Barnacle-owned candidate labels (`candidateLabelValues`); `bad-barnacle` and privileged-author skips preserved; full test suite covers all paths.

target.title: fix(cli): avoid false local gateway unreachable on probe timeout
target.body:
## Summary
- separate local gateway reachability from follow-up detail/read probe failures
- add a bounded direct status-RPC fallback or degraded classification for local status/probe paths when the default probe times out or returns unknown capability but the gateway is otherwise RPC-capable
- add regression coverage for the #73535 split: default local probe false-negative while `--require-rpc` or direct `status` RPC succeeds

## Scope

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches

target.title: fix(commitments): stop hardcoding fastMode in extraction (#78451)
target.body:
## Summary

MiniMax Coding Plan operators whose plan owns `MiniMax-M2.7` but not the `-highspeed` variant hit `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` on every commitment extraction pass, because the commitments extraction path passed `fastMode: true` unconditionally to `runEmbeddedPiAgent`, which caused the MiniMax stream wrapper's `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` to rewrite the base model id to `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`.

## Root Cause

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts:242` (pre-fix): the call to `runEmbeddedPiAgent` for hidden commitment extraction hardcoded `fastMode: true`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24`: the MiniMax stream wrapper accepts a `fastMode` boolean and, when true, swaps the base model id to its `-highspeed` variant.
- Result: every commitments extraction request silently flipped `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, which Coding Plan operators do not own. MiniMax responds with HTTP 500 / error code 2061. Reporter verified the Coding Plan owns the base model but not highspeed.
- The reporter listed three valid fix shapes (drop the hardcode, add a config field, or inherit agent fastMode). This PR uses option 1: dropping the hardcode lets the existing `resolveFastModeState` helper continue to control fast-mode for the rest of the agent runtime, and commitments extraction inherits the same default-false behavior.

## Changes

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts`: remove `fastMode: true` from the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload built in the commitments extraction path. Add a comment explaining the reason so this does not regress.
- `src/commitments/runtime.test.ts`: extend the existing `runEmbeddedPiAgentMock` assertion to assert `fastMode` is **not** in the payload, locking in the regression fix.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: single-line entry under Unreleased / Fixes.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: On every commitment extraction pass, MiniMax Coding Plan operators receive `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` from the MiniMax server because the agent runtime requested `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` even though their plan only owns `MiniMax-M2.7`. The reporter (`@advaitpaliwal`) verified `commitments.enabled: true, commitments.maxPerDay: 10` config, then ran a real Coding Plan session, observed extraction always selecting the highspeed variant, and traced the root cause to `dist/runtime-CXt7BFiH.js` line 386 (`fastMode: true` hardcoded). Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78451
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-78451` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `src/commitments/runtime.ts` directly from the worktree**, locates the entire `defaultExtractBatch` function (the path that builds the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload), prints its SHA-256 + verbatim bytes, then runs structural assertions over the captured payload literal to prove `fastMode: true` is gone, the `#78451` explanatory comment is in place, and every sibling field is preserved. No mocks, no test runner — the patched source bytes drive the smoke. PR head: `a3189f329687fa8c2c9da783afbc0d644ea94573`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git checkout fix/commitments-no-fastmode-hardcode && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts`, where `smoke-pr78459.mts` reads `src/commitments/runtime.ts`, slices the `async function defaultExtractBatch` body, hashes it, prints the verbatim payload object literal, and runs structural assertions against the live patched source.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `a3189f3296` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `src/commitments/runtime.ts` `defaultExtractBatch` function read from the worktree, 1660 bytes, SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts
=== Patched defaultExtractBatch function — bytes from src/commitments/runtime.ts ===
offset: 7245 -> 8905 ( 1660 bytes)
SHA-256: ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379

function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false
explanatory '#78451' comment present                : true
  preserved field 'disableTools: true': true
  preserved field 'thinkLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'verboseLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'reasoningLevel: "off"': true

=== Payload object passed to runEmbeddedPiAgent (verbatim from patched code) ===
{ cfg, agentId: first.agentId });
  const result = await runEmbeddedPiAgent({
    sessionId: runId,
    sessionKey: `agent:${first.agentId}:commitments:${runId}`,
    agentId: first.agentId,
    trigger: "manual",
    sessionFile: resolveExtractionSessionFile(first.agentId, runId),
    workspaceDir: resolveAgentWorkspaceDir(cfg, first.agentId),
    config: cfg,
    provider: modelRef.provider,
    model: modelRef.model,
    prompt: buildCommitmentExtractionPrompt({ cfg, items: params.items }),
    disableTools: true,
    thinkLevel: "off",
    verboseLevel: "off",
    reasoningLevel: "off",
    // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
    // commitments extraction inherit the configured fast-mode state (which
    // defaults to false). Hardcoding true caused MiniMax Coding Plans without
    // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
    // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
    timeoutMs: resolved.extraction.timeoutSeconds * 1000,
    runId,
    bootstrapContextMode: "lightweight",
    skillsSnapshot: { prompt: "", skills: [] },
    suppressToolErrorWarnings: true,
  }

=== fastMode-related lines in the payload (BEFORE-vs-AFTER comparison) ===
  // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
  // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
  // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
~~~

- Observed result after fix: The patched `defaultExtractBatch` function — pinned by a 1660-byte / SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379` integrity check — has zero `fastMode: true` literals (`function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false`). The explanatory `#78451` comment is in place (`explanatory '#78451' comment present: true`), and every sibling field (`disableTools: true`, `thinkLevel: "off"`, `verboseLevel: "off"`, `reasoningLevel: "off"`) is preserved verbatim. The captured payload literal shown verbatim above confirms `runEmbeddedPiAgent` is now called without a `fastMode` key — so the MiniMax stream wrapper at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24` no longer flips `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, and the operator's configured base model id flows through unchanged.
- What was not tested: A live HTTP roundtrip against the real MiniMax server with a Coding Plan token is not available on the contributor machine, so this is source-level / structural proof against the patched payload-builder, not a live MiniMax 200 response. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live MiniMax recording is required; the reporter's original issue already contains live HTTP 500 evidence from the real Coding Plan.

## Test

- `pnpm test src/commitments/runtime.test.ts` — 6 passed (including the new `expect(callArgs).not.toHaveProperty("fastMode")` regression assertion).

## Notes

- Scope: smallest complete fix. No new config surface and no provider/wrapper edits — the existing fast-mode resolution chain already covers the right behavior; the hardcode was the only thing forcing `true`.
- Compatibility: agents that need fast-mode for commitments can still set it via `agents.defaults.models.<id>.params.fastMode: true` (or per-agent `fastModeDefault: true`) — the `resolveFastModeState` chain is unchanged.

Closes #78451

target.title: fix(barnacle): resync candidate labels on ready_for_review, remove stale labels
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` return value + `removeLabels` call path
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a new coverage gap
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a maintainer-owned PR transitions from draft to `ready_for_review` (or is edited), Barnacle recomputes candidate labels and removes any stale Barnacle-owned candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) that no longer match the PR body/files.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [add missing labels only] -> [stale labels persist]

After:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [recompute labels] -> [add missing + remove stale] -> [labels match body]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No (existing GitHub API calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any (GitHub Actions)
- Runtime/container: Node 24 (GitHub Actions)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: GitHub auto-response workflow

### Steps

1. Open a draft PR with a blank issue template body
2. Barnacle auto-labels it with `triage: blank-template`
3. Edit body to fill in the template (Problem/Why/What)
4. Click "Ready for review"

### Expected

- `triage: blank-template` is removed; correct candidate labels applied

### Actual

- `triage: blank-template` persisted (before fix)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `ready_for_review` with meaningful body removes stale labels; `ready_for_review` recomputes candidate labels; `bad-barnacle` suppresses removal; maintainer-authored PRs not mutated
- Edge cases checked: `bad-barnacle` suppression, maintainer/OWNER/COLLABORATOR skip, mixed candidate label transitions
- What you did **not** verify: Live GitHub workflow run (requires merge to test end-to-end)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Stale label removal could incorrectly strip a label if `classifyPullRequestCandidateLabels` has a bug.
  - Mitigation: Removal only targets Barnacle-owned candidate labels (`candidateLabelValues`); `bad-barnacle` and privileged-author skips preserved; full test suite covers all paths.

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- packed this branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball into a fresh npm project, and verified `npm audit --omit=dev --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches
target.author: alaleiwang
